app.name: Storico

landing.hero_subtitle: Automate the decomposition of user stories into structured Kanban tasks using AI.

hero.title: From user stories to technical tasks in seconds
hero.subtitle: Paste a natural language user story and get structured tasks on a Kanban board, ready to export to Trello, JSON, or Markdown.

features.title: Everything you need to manage your backlog
features.subtitle: From automatic extraction to exporting to your favorite tool.
features.card_1_desc: Paste a user story and instantly get structured technical tasks powered by AI.
features.card_2_desc: Visualize and organize your tasks on a Kanban board with drag & drop.
features.card_3_desc: Export your tasks to Trello, JSON, or Markdown with one click. Compatible with your existing workflow.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Storico.
faq.a1: Storico is an open-source tool that uses AI to automatically break down user stories into structured Kanban tasks. Just paste a user story and get technical tasks with descriptions, labels, and dependencies ready to export.
faq.q2: Do I need a credit card or API key to use it?
faq.a2: Not at all. Storico is free and open source. You can run it locally using Ollama with no API keys, no subscriptions, and no hidden costs.
faq.q3: What kind of user stories does it work with?
faq.a3: Storico works best with standard "As a... I want... so that..." format user stories in English. The more specific your story, the better the task breakdown.
faq.q4: Can I edit the generated tasks before exporting?
faq.a4: Absolutely. You can review, edit, or delete any task before exporting. You also have full control over the resulting Kanban board.
faq.q5: What formats can I export to?
faq.a5: You can export to Trello (directly to your boards), JSON, or Markdown. CSV and XML support are on the roadmap.
faq.q6: Can I use local AI models instead of cloud APIs?
faq.a6: Yes. Storico supports Ollama out of the box, so you can run entirely offline with local models like LLaMA 3.2 or Mistral. Cloud models (OpenAI, Anthropic) are also supported.

about.title: About Storico
about.description: Learn about Storico, its mission, and the team behind it.
about.intro_p1: Storico is an open-source tool that leverages Large Language Models to automatically decompose user stories into structured, actionable Kanban tasks. It was developed as part of a Master's thesis in Software Engineering at the
about.mission: Our mission is to bridge the gap between requirements expressed in natural language and actionable development tasks. We believe that AI should augment — not replace — the planning skills of agile teams, reducing cognitive load and eliminating misinterpretations.
about.problem: 66% of software projects partially or fully fail (CHAOS Report 2020), and 58% of agile teams use user stories as their primary requirements source. Manually breaking down stories into tasks is inconsistent, tedious, and error-prone. Storico automates this process, providing consistent, high-quality task decomposition every time.
about.team_p2: as part of his Master's research, advised by
about.team_advisor: Dr. Carlos Alberto Fernández y Fernández

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: How Storico handles your data and protects your privacy.
privacy.intro: Your privacy matters. This policy explains what data Storico handles, why, and the choices you have. It covers both the open source engine and the Storico Cloud service.
privacy.self_hosted_body: Storico is open source software that runs on your own machines. The open source engine contains no telemetry, no analytics, and no phone-home. When you run Storico locally with a local LLM (such as Ollama), your data never leaves your machine and we receive no data about your usage.
privacy.collection_oauth: When you sign in via Google or GitHub OAuth, we receive your email address and display name. We do
privacy.collection_oauth_end: store passwords — authentication is handled entirely by your OAuth provider.
privacy.collection_llm: The user stories you submit are sent to the configured LLM for task extraction. If you use a local model via Ollama, your data never leaves your machine. If you use a cloud provider (OpenAI, Anthropic), your data is processed by them according to their respective policies. We do not use your content to train models.
privacy.collection_communications: If you contact us via email or GitHub issues, we keep the messages and details you provide so we can respond and manage the relationship.
privacy.providers_intro: We use a small number of providers to make Storico work. They process data only on our instructions and under contract.
privacy.providers_note: Cloud LLM providers are only involved if you choose to configure them. When using a local model via Ollama, no data is sent to third parties.
privacy.usage_1: To provide the task extraction service you requested
privacy.usage_2: To improve extraction quality via anonymized usage patterns
privacy.usage_3: To respond to your requests and provide support
privacy.legal_body: Where the GDPR or similar laws apply, we rely on these bases: performance of a contract to provide the service you asked for; legitimate interests to secure the service and prevent abuse; and legal obligation where the law requires us to retain or disclose data.
privacy.security_body: We protect data with measures appropriate to its sensitivity. Login sessions are handled via secure OAuth flows. We do not store passwords. No system is perfectly secure, but we work to keep the bar high and respond quickly to issues.
privacy.retention: You retain full ownership of your data. Projects, stories, and tasks can be deleted at any time from within the application. Account data is kept for as long as you have an account. Usage data is kept only as long as needed to operate the service and meet legal requirements. We do not sell or share your personal information with third parties.
privacy.transfers_body: If you choose to use a cloud LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic), your data may be processed in countries other than your own. Those transfers are governed by the respective provider's data processing agreements. When running Storico locally, no data transfer occurs.
privacy.rights_body: Depending on where you live, you may have the right to access, correct, delete, or port your personal data, to object to or restrict certain processing, and to withdraw consent. If you are in the EEA or UK, the GDPR applies. If you are a California resident, the CCPA/CPRA applies, including the right to know, delete, and opt out of sale (which we do not do). To exercise these rights, email us at alvaldes.dev@gmail.com. We will respond within the period the law requires. You also have the right to complain to your local data protection authority.
privacy.cookies_body: This website uses no tracking or advertising cookies. The dashboard uses strictly necessary session cookies to keep you signed in. These cookies are not used for advertising or cross-site tracking.
privacy.children_body: Storico is a tool for software development teams and is not directed to children. We do not knowingly collect personal data from anyone under 16. If you believe a child has provided us with data, contact us and we will delete it.
privacy.changes_body: We may update this policy as Storico evolves. We will revise the 'Last updated' date above and, for material changes, give reasonable notice through the application or by email.
privacy.contact_before: For any privacy question or request, email us at
privacy.contact_after: or open an issue on our GitHub repository.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: Terms and conditions for using Storico.
terms.intro: By using Storico, you agree to these terms.
terms.service: Storico is provided “as is” without any warranty. You are responsible for reviewing and validating the generated tasks before using them in any production or mission-critical environment.
terms.opensource_p2: You are free to use, modify, and distribute the software in accordance with the terms of that license.
terms.liability: Storico and its contributors shall not be liable for any damages arising from the use or inability to use the software. This includes, but is not limited to, incorrect task decomposition, data loss, or project delays.
terms.changes: We reserve the right to update these terms at any time. Continued use of Storico after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.description: Learn how to use Storico with guides, tutorials, and references.
docs.welcome: Welcome to Storico's documentation. This section will be expanded with detailed guides, tutorials, and reference materials.
docs.step_1: Run Storico locally — clone the repository and follow the setup guide in the README.
docs.step_2: Configure your LLM — choose between Ollama (local), OpenAI, or Anthropic. Ollama works out of the box with no API key.
docs.step_3: Create a project — organize your user stories into projects.
docs.step_4: Paste a user story — use the standard “As a… I want… so that…” format.
docs.step_5: Extract tasks — let AI break down your story into structured tasks.
docs.step_6: Review and export — edit tasks as needed and export to Trello, JSON, or Markdown.
docs.story_format_intro: Storico works best with stories in standard format:
docs.story_format_example: As a(n) [role], I want [feature], so that [benefit]
docs.story_format_example_text: “As a user, I want to reset my password so that I can regain access to my account.”
docs.llm_backend_ollama: Ollama — LLaMA 3.2, Mistral, and other local models
docs.more_coming: More detailed documentation, including API guides and architecture overview, coming soon.

api.endpoint_status_desc: Check the status of an async batch extraction.
api.footer: For detailed request/response schemas, start the backend and visit
